body:
## Summary
- Add explicit `@sepo-agent /setup plan` routing for issue-backed setup planning.
- Run the setup route with read-approved permissions and skip status-label/assignee mutations for setup plans.
- Document setup route access, plan-only boundaries, and update route/rubric metadata.
- Add focused tests for parsing, access policy, workflow boundaries, and docs consistency.

## Verification
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/triage.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/issue-template.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/rubrics.test.js`
- `git diff --check`

Closes #146

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| Source | Next | Target | Round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| implement | review | PR #147 | 3 / 40 | Dispatched |

Reason: implementation succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777936495673 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUzNDgyOTk3NTc6aW1wbGVtZW50OjE0NjpyZXZpZXc6MTQ3OjM -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 88 | PASS | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | partial | 4/7 | The setup route posts via `post-response.js` as a normal issue response, with no stable setup-plan marker or update/reuse lookup shown in the PR diff. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The PR adds a distinct `/setup plan` route, `setup` envelope/route values, and `route_overrides.setup` access-policy documentation instead of overloading another route. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate preview contracts | partial | 5/7 | The prompt, workflow, and tests enforce plan-only boundaries and issue-only routing, but the setup plan output remains agent-written markdown without deterministic validation of the proposed diff structure. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | Issue #146 is explicitly staged under parent #126 and base PR #145; the PR preserves the staged boundary by adding only plan behavior and deferring apply/mutation work. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to route registration, access policy, router workflow, setup prompt, docs, and focused tests for the new setup-plan route. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | `access-policy.ts` adds a conservative setup default excluding `CONTRIBUTOR`, tests cover default denial, and `agent-router.yml` runs setup only when the resolved route is `setup` and target kind is `issue`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The workflow reuses existing shared actions (`resolve-github-auth`, `resolve-agent-provider`, `setup-agent-runtime`, `run-agent-task`) and existing `post-response.js` posting flow. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | The PR updates access policy, supported workflows, action docs, quick start, key concepts, project planning, and rubric route docs for the new setup route. |

## Notes

- Strongest rubric alignment is around route authorization, staged scope control, docs coverage, and reuse of existing router/runtime patterns.
- The main rubric-specific gap is idempotency for rerun setup plans: a repeated `/setup plan` appears likely to create another visible issue comment rather than update a prior setup-plan artifact.
- No rubric-specific follow-up is required before shipping, but the smallest useful improvement would be a stable hidden marker or lookup/update path for setup-plan comments.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** The new setup-plan route creates user-facing setup guidance but does not appear idempotent. The PR adds `Post setup plan` using `node .agent/dist/cli/post-response.js`; the existing response flow posts ordinary issue comments, and the PR does not add a setup-specific marker, collapse, or update path. This partially misses `idempotent-user-facing-automation`.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #147 implements the staged `@sepo-agent /setup plan` route from issue #146 under parent issue #126. It adds route parsing, access-policy defaults, envelope/rubric route metadata, a setup planning prompt, router workflow wiring, docs, and focused tests. The route is intentionally issue-only and plan-only: it reads setup intent from the setup issue template added in PR #145 and must not mutate repository variables, GitHub Projects, labels, assignees, or files. `/setup apply`, Project creation/linking, variable writes, and Project field sync remain intentionally out of scope.

## Review
The PR is directionally aligned with the staged plan, but one authorization regression blocks the primary issue-comment workflow for this public repo. Reviewers disagreed on ship readiness: Claude judged the PR shippable, rubrics passed with warnings, while Codex flagged a concrete blocking access issue that live repo data reproduces.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Setup route denies verified maintainers on issue comments | BLOCKING | The setup default excludes `CONTRIBUTOR`, but this public repo reports `lolipopshock` issue comments as `CONTRIBUTOR` even though collaborator permission is `admin`. Because weak-association refresh only runs for `issues` events, an authorized maintainer’s `/setup plan` issue comment can resolve to `unsupported`. |
| Setup plan posting lacks deterministic preview safeguards | WARNING | The setup job posts agent-written markdown directly through `post-response.js`, so malformed or overbroad setup plans are still presented as successful previews. Repeated `/setup plan` runs also appear likely to create fresh comments rather than update a stable setup-plan artifact. |

## Progress
- The PR correctly preserves the child scope: plan-only setup route now, deterministic apply/mutation later.
- Docs and tests were updated across route parsing, workflow boundaries, access policy, prompt scope, and supported workflows.
- The rubrics review already posted a PASS with partial notes on idempotency and preview-contract validation.
- No inline comments existed before synthesis; one inline comment was posted for the blocking access issue on `.agent/src/access-policy.ts`.
- Optional reviewer notes about parser comments, empty-intersection coverage, and regex fragility are not treated as required branch changes.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Setup route denies verified maintainers on issue comments</summary>

**Cause:** [`DEFAULT_ROUTE_ALLOWED_ASSOCIATIONS`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/07415768563b8cc94bf82ed1e816f987259611c1/.agent/src/access-policy.ts#L28-L30) excludes `CONTRIBUTOR`, which is the intended conservative setup default. However, [`extract-context.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/07415768563b8cc94bf82ed1e816f987259611c1/.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts#L113-L120) only refreshes weak associations for `issues` events, so `issue_comment` triggers keep GitHub’s reported `CONTRIBUTOR` association even for verified collaborators/admins.

**Candidate solutions:** Refresh `issue_comment` author permission before route policy is applied, or map verified repository collaborators/admins to `COLLABORATOR` for the dispatch policy path. Add regression coverage for a public-repo `issue_comment` from a verified collaborator reported as `CONTRIBUTOR`, while preserving denial for unverified contributors.

**Comments:** Codex marked this blocking; Claude did not raise it. The synthesis treats it as blocking because live repo checks show `lolipopshock` has `admin` collaborator permission while issue/comment payloads report `CONTRIBUTOR`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Setup plan posting lacks deterministic preview safeguards</summary>

**Cause:** [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/07415768563b8cc94bf82ed1e816f987259611c1/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml#L469-L477) posts the setup agent response directly. The prompt defines allowed setup intent and mutation boundaries, but there is no deterministic validation step or stable marker/update path before the comment becomes the user-visible setup preview.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a small validator before posting that checks required sections, allowlisted fields, and forbidden write/apply content, surfacing a failure or explicit warning when the output violates the preview contract. Add a hidden marker or lookup/update path so repeated `/setup plan` runs update or reuse the prior setup-plan comment.

**Comments:** Codex raised this as a warning, and rubrics flagged both preview-contract and idempotency gaps. Claude considered the current plan-only route acceptable because `approve-reads` enforces the mutation boundary, so this remains non-blocking.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: automation can safely patch the blocking authorization regression and add focused coverage, while maintainers can separately decide whether preview validation/idempotent posting must land in this PR.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Fix setup-route authorization so verified collaborators/admins whose public issue comments report `CONTRIBUTOR` can still run `/setup plan`, with regression coverage.

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| Source | Next | Target | Round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| review | fix-pr | PR #147 | 4 / 40 | Dispatched |

Reason: review verdict is needs_rework; dispatching fix-pr

Task for fix-pr:
Address only the latest review synthesis action items:
- Fix setup-route authorization so verified collaborators/admins whose public issue comments report `CONTRIBUTOR` can still run `/setup plan`, with regression coverage.

Constraints: Ignore optional INFO notes, metadata-only polish, already-fixed findings, and human-judgment nits unless required by those action items.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777937129936 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUzNDg3MDE1NTY6cmV2aWV3OjE0NzpmaXhfcHI6MTQ3OjQ -->

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-146/codex-25348299757`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

- Fixed setup authorization by promoting weak `issue_comment` associations to `COLLABORATOR` after a repository collaborator check, so verified collaborators/admins can run `/setup plan` when public comments report `CONTRIBUTOR`.
- Added regression coverage for a public issue-comment `/setup plan` request from a verified collaborator and documented the issue-comment refresh behavior.
- Verification: `npm --prefix .agent run build`; `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/extract-context-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/triage.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/resolve-dispatch-cli.test.js`; `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`; `git diff --check`.

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| Source | Next | Target | Round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| fix-pr | review | PR #147 | 5 / 40 | Dispatched |

Reason: PR fixes succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777937480044 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUzNDkwNzA1MjQ6Zml4X3ByOjE0NzpyZXZpZXc6MTQ3OjU -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 91 | PASS | 8 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | partial | 4/7 | The setup job posts through `post-response.js`; existing collapse/update behavior is limited to rubrics reviews, with no setup-plan marker or reuse path added. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Adds a distinct `setup` route and only recognizes explicit `/setup plan`, while `/setup` and `/setup apply` remain unsupported. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate preview contracts | partial | 5/7 | The route runs with `approve-reads` and the prompt forbids writes, but posted plan markdown is not deterministically validated before becoming the preview. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR scope matches issue #146 under parent #126: plan-only setup route now, apply/Project mutations deferred. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to route parsing, access policy, context extraction, router workflow, setup prompt, docs, and focused tests. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | `setup` has a narrower default allowlist, issue-comment collaborator promotion was added, and tests cover verified collaborator setup access. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Reuses existing router, provider/runtime actions, task runner, envelope validation, and `post-response.js` flow. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates access policy, supported workflows, action docs, quick start, key concepts, project planning, and rubric route docs. |

## Notes

- The follow-up fix resolves the prior authorization blocker: weak public `issue_comment` associations from verified collaborators are promoted before setup route policy is applied.
- Remaining rubric gaps are non-blocking and both relate to the plan-preview surface: repeated `/setup plan` runs are not idempotent, and the agent-written plan has no deterministic output validator.
- Smallest useful follow-up: add a stable setup-plan marker/update path and a focused validator for required sections plus forbidden apply/write content.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** Setup-plan comments are not idempotent. Re-running `/setup plan` appears to create another visible issue comment because the PR uses the generic `post-response.js` path without a setup-specific marker, lookup, or update behavior.
- **WARNING:** The preview contract is prompt-enforced rather than validator-enforced. `approve-reads` limits tool permissions, but malformed or overbroad setup-plan markdown can still be posted as the successful preview.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #147 adds the staged, issue-only `@sepo-agent /setup plan` route requested by issue #146 under parent issue #126. It wires parsing, access policy, setup prompt behavior, router execution, docs, and focused tests for a plan-only setup flow. The route is intentionally read-only: `/setup apply`, repo variable writes, GitHub Project creation/linking, and Project field sync remain deferred.

## Review
The follow-up fix resolves the prior authorization blocker, and the reviewers now agree there is no required branch-change work before shipping. Reviewer severity differed on setup-plan preview/idempotency hardening: rubrics and Claude call it warning-level future work, while Codex treats it as acceptable INFO for this staged plan-only child.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No unresolved actionable issues | INFO | The previous setup-route authorization regression is fixed. Remaining notes are non-blocking future hardening for idempotent setup-plan comments, deterministic plan validation, and an optional negative test case. |

## Progress
- Prior blocker fixed: weak public `issue_comment` associations are now promoted after collaborator verification before setup route policy runs.
- PR scope remains aligned with #146/#126: `/setup plan` only, with setup apply and mutation paths intentionally out of scope.
- Docs and tests cover route parsing, access policy, workflow boundaries, prompt scope, and issue-comment auth refresh.
- Existing inline feedback already covered the earlier auth issue; no new non-duplicative inline comment was warranted.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: no unresolved required branch-change work remains, so automation should stop and maintainers can proceed with normal merge judgment.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

title: Add plan-only setup route
